seoTitle: How to Scan a PayPal QR Code Easily (2026)
metaDescription: See how to use PayPal's built-in scanner to pay at checkout by scanning a merchant's QR code, choosing a funding source, and confirming payment.
intro: Contactless checkouts at markets, food carts, or in-store registers are faster when you use PayPal's QR scanner instead of typing in payment details manually. This guide covers opening the scanner, granting camera access, and completing a scan-to-pay transaction. It's aimed at anyone who wants a quick, touch-free way to pay in person.

steps.heading: Open the PayPal app and find the scanner
steps.body: Launch the PayPal app and tap the QR code icon near the top of your home screen to open the scan-to-pay tool.

steps.heading: Allow camera access
steps.body: If prompted, grant camera permissions so the app can link your phone's camera to your wallet for scanning.

steps.heading: Line up the merchant's code
steps.body: Point your camera at the printed or on-screen QR code and hold steady until the app auto-detects it and pulls up the checkout screen.

steps.heading: Confirm the payee and amount
steps.body: Check that the checkout screen shows the correct merchant, then enter the amount you owe if it isn't pre-filled.

steps.heading: Pick a funding source and send
steps.body: Swipe to your preferred card or balance, then tap send to authorize the payment and show the confirmation screen to the merchant.

faq.question: What if the QR code won't scan?
faq.answer: Try adjusting the distance and angle to your phone, or make sure the code isn't glared out by overhead lighting.

faq.question: Can I use a debit card instead of my PayPal balance?
faq.answer: Yes, you can swipe through your linked funding sources and pick any card or balance before sending payment.

faq.question: Is there a fee to scan and pay in person?
faq.answer: In most cases scan-to-pay works like a standard PayPal payment, so normal PayPal fees apply depending on the funding source.

faq.question: Do I need an internet connection to scan a code?
faq.answer: Yes, your phone needs an active data or Wi-Fi connection since the transaction is processed through PayPal's servers in real time.
